steps.title: Calculate the slope of line k from two points on the graph
steps.content:
Line $k$ passes through $(-5, 1)$ and $(1, -4)$. Using the slope formula:

$$m_k = \frac{-4-1}{1-(-5)} = \frac{-5}{6}$$

steps.title: Apply the perpendicular slope relationship
steps.content:
Perpendicular lines have slopes that are negative reciprocals. If line $k$ has slope $-\frac{5}{6}$, then line $j$ has slope:

$$m_j = -\frac{1}{\left(-\frac{5}{6}\right)} = \frac{6}{5}$$

The slope of line $j$ is $\frac{6}{5}$ or equivalently \$1.2$.
